sections.paragraph:
with a distinct. accent. רָע, pl. רָעִים (from the root רָעַע).

(A) fem. רָעָה adj. evil, bad-(a) physically as of an animal, bad cattle, Lev 27:10 Deu 17:1 bad waters, 2Ki 2:19, etc.-(b) ἠθικῶς, wicked, evil, of the manner of thinking and acting, Gen 6:5 8:21 1Sa 25:3. לֵב רָע an evil heart, Jer 3:17 7:24 . רַע בְּעֵינֵי פ׳ i.e. what is unpleasing to any one, Gen 38:7 often in this connection, הָרַע בְּעֵינֵי יְהֹוָה what was displeasing to Jehovah, 1Ki 11:6 14:22 more rarely followed by עַל Ecc 2:17 followed by לִפְנֵי Neh 2:1. Specially-(aa) noxious, hurtful. חַיָּה רָעָה an evil beast, Gen 37:33. דָּבָר רָע an evil, i.e. a hurtful thing, 2Ki 4:41.-(bb) רַע עַיִן envious, malignant, Pro 23:6 28:22 .

(2) evil in appearance, deformed, especially when followed by מַרְאֶה Gen 41:3.

(3) unhappy, unfortunate, of a person, Isa 3:11 compare verse 10 . Jer 7:6, לְרַע לָכֶם “that it may go ill with you,” for לִהְיֹות רַע לָכֶם (compare טֹוב No. 1), Jer 25:7.

(4) sad, of the heart or mind, Pro 25:20 of the countenance, Gen 40:7 Neh 2:2.

(B) subst. evil, badness, especially in an ethical sense, τὸ κακόν, Gen 2:9 Deu 22:22 עֲצַת רָע evil counsel, Eze 11:2 אַנְשֵׁי רַע evil men, Pro 28:5 also wickedness, malice, Gen 6:5. In this sense there is more frequently used the fem. רָעָה, which see.

I. רֵעַ (from the root ר֯וּעַ, noise, outcry (Lerm), Exo 32:17 Mic 4:9. The noise of God is thunder, Job 36:33, יַגִּיד עָלָיו רֵעֹו “he declares to him (to man or to his enemy) his thunder.”

II. רֵעַ (from the root רָעָה No. 3) for the more full רֵעֶה, with suff. רֵעִי, רֵעֲךָ, רֵעֹו Jerem. 6:21 but much more in use is רֵעֵהוּ, plur. רֵעִים; with suff. רֵעֶיךָ; רֵעֵהוּ (for רֵעֵיהוּ Job 42:10 1Sa 30:26); רֵעֵיהֶם m. a companion, a friend, with whom one has intercourse, Job 2:11 19:21 Pro 25:17 implying less than אֹהֵב Pro 18:24. Followed by dat. (like the Greek ὁ ἐμοὶ φίλος), Job 30:29, רֵעַ לִבְנֹות יַעֲנָה “a companion (i.e. like) to ostriches.” Pro 19:6. Friend or companion is also said of-(a) a lover, one beloved of a woman. Son 5:16 Jer 3:1, 20 Hos 3:1 compare רַעְיָה.-(b) any other person, any other of the human race, ὁ πλησίον (Nächfter, Mitmenfch), Exo 20:17, seqq.; 22:25 . Hence when preceded by אִישׁ, alter, alter, one, another. Jdg 6:29, וַיּאֹמְרוּ אִישׁ אֶל־רֵעֵהוּ “they said one to another.” Gen 11:3 1Sa 10:11 20:41 . Also used of inanimate things, Gen 15:10. Compare אָח No. 6. More rarely when not preceded by אִישׁ Isai. 34:14 , שָׂעִיר אֶל־רֵעֵהוּ יִקְרָא “satyr shall cry to satyr.”

(2) thought, will, i.q. Ch. רְעוּת, רַעְיֹון Psa 139:2, 17 Psa 139:17. Root רְעָה Ch. to will, and ܪܥܐܳ Ethpa. to think.
